content: Just as structured guided reflection exercises in high English language learning improve students' ability to revise awkward, unclear writing by asking them to compare their draft against alternative perspectives, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow this classroom framework to improve reasoning outputs by having models explicitly compare their initial solutions against multiple alternative responses to identify logical gaps. This turns the abstract technical concept of LLM self-reflection into a replicable pedagogy-inspired routine that is already proven to work for human learners.
